documentation.text: @notice Sequential mapping of batch IDs to their anchor records.
@notice Address authorised to submit anchors (e.g. the Raspberry Pi wallet).
@notice Emitted every time a new Merkle root is anchored.

@notice Submit a Merkle root for a new batch of safety events.
 @param _merkleRoot  The 32-byte Merkle root of the event batch.

the Merkle root is stored on-chain \u2014 not raw event data.      This minimises gas costs while preserving full cryptographic      verifiability through Merkle proofs computed off-chain.      Architecture: Hybrid Anchoring        Local layer  \u2192 full event data + local blockchain + Merkle trees        Public layer \u2192 Merkle root + timestamp + batch_id (this contract)","kind":"dev","methods":{"constructor":{"params":{"_submitter":"Address allowed to call submitAnchor()."}},"getAnchor(uint256)":{"params":{"_batchId":"The sequential batch
